channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Band e TV Cultura fecham acordo para realização de debates à Presidência nas eleições
item.description: Band and TV Cultura have reached an agreement to jointly produce debates with presidential candidates in the 2026 elections. The partnership, negotiated since the beginning of the year, was confirmed on March 17, 2026. The full article is behind a paywall, requiring subscription or access granted by subscribers.

item.title: Com julgamento no TSE, Castro avalia saída antecipada
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) is considering an early resignation to avoid impeachment by the TSE after Justice Nunes Marques requested a delay in the case. His legal team argues that resigning before the trial concludes would render the case moot, but experts warn that ineligibility could still apply if his direct involvement in wrongdoing is proven. A second option to step down by April 4 depends on confidence he will avoid both removal and ineligibility, amid growing perceptions that political rivals see him as less antagonistic toward the Supreme Court.

item.title: Vereador anuncia Flávio em São Caetano, mas não combina com pré-candidato
item.description: A councilman announced support for pre-candidate Flávio in São Caetano do Sul, but the announcement was not coordinated or endorsed by the pre-candidate himself, creating a local campaign discrepancy. The episode highlights a lack of alignment between local leaders and the official pre-candidacy strategy, potentially undermining the political group's cohesion.

item.title: Filipe Martins apresenta recurso ao STF e pede anulação de condenação
item.description: Filipe Martins, former advisor to Jair Bolsonaro, filed a petition with the STF seeking to annul his 21-year prison sentence for involvement in the coup plot. His defense claims the Court relied on false evidence from the Federal Police and challenges Mauro Cid’s testimony, while also arguing the STF lacks jurisdiction due to absence of privileged forum.

item.title: Primeira-ministra do Peru renuncia três semanas depois de assumir o cargo e antes das eleições
item.description: Peru's Prime Minister Denisse Miralles resigned three weeks after taking office, just one day before she was scheduled to seek a confidence vote in Congress. Her resignation comes less than a month before the April 12 general elections, further deepening political instability in a country that has seen eight presidents since 2018 due to resignations and removals. The move also coincides with Peru's worst energy crisis in two decades, triggered by a gas pipeline rupture that forced natural gas rationing and disrupted industry.

item.title: Esposa do prefeito Ricardo Nunes oficializa filiação ao MDB
item.description: Regina Carnovale Nunes, wife of São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes, officially joined the MDB party in March 2026, targeting the 2026 elections. She described the move as a 'conscious step' to continue working on social causes, family strengthening, and building a more just and humane city. Her affiliation comes amid a civil inquiry into alleged administrative impropriety involving the mayor and a rented property tied to unlicensed contracts with the city, which yielded R$624 million to a construction firm between 2021 and 2024.

item.title: Valdemar barra alianças do PL com o PT nas eleições de 2026
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the Liberal Party (PL), banned state directors from forming alliances with left-wing parties, such as the Workers' Party (PT), for the 2026 elections. The decision aims to avoid repeating the 2024 scenario, when the PL formed alliances with the PT in several states, drawing internal criticism.

item.title: PT culpa Bolsonaro pelo Master e reage a casos que afetam o Planalto
item.description: The PT intensified its attacks against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, linking him to the Banco Master case and the Bolsonarist legacy in a resolution by the National Executive. The party frames the 2026 election as a choice between a popular, democratic project and a neoliberal, authoritarian one, highlighting Lula’s economic achievements. The strategy includes strengthening the political defense of the government against scandals involving allies, such as Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, through greater PT and congressional engagement.

item.title: Fátima Bezerra anuncia que permanece no governo do RN até o fim do mandato
item.description: Rio Grande do Norte Governor Fátima Bezerra (PT) announced she will remain in office until the end of her term in December 2026, ruling out a potential resignation to run for the Senate in 2026. She cited a political rupture with Vice Governor Walter Alves (MDB), who refused to assume the governorship if she resigned and endorsed her political rival, Allyson Bezerra (União Brasil). Her continued tenure eliminates the need for the state legislature to appoint an interim governor.

item.title: Governadores negam pedido de Lula sobre redução de ICMS sobre combustíveis
item.description: Governors, through Comsefaz, rejected President Lula's request to reduce the ICMS tax on fuels, arguing it would harm the financing of state public policies. The group stated that tax cuts would compromise states' ability to maintain essential services.

item.title: Exclusivo: relator da CPMI do INSS vai pedir indiciamento de Lulinha
item.description: The rapporteur of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission (CPMI), Deputy Alfredo Gaspar (União-LA), intends to request the indictment of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, over alleged involvement in a fraud scheme targeting retirement and pension benefits. The son of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is accused of participating in the misappropriation of social security funds. Legal implications are still under review.

item.title: Kassab indica a aliados que anunciará Ratinho Jr. como pré-candidato do PSD
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab signaled to allies that he will announce Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. as the party's pre-candidate for presidency next week. Three party leaders confirmed the tendency, naming him as the internal favorite, though they avoid finalizing the decision due to potential last-minute changes in discussions.

item.title: Congresso Nacional promulga acordo entre Mercosul e União Europeia
item.description: Brazil's National Congress promulgated the free trade agreement between Mercosul and the European Union, which will reduce or eliminate tariffs on over 90% of trade between the blocs and cover a market of 700 million people. The treaty, approved by the Federal Senate on March 4 following a vote in the Chamber of Deputies, requires ratification by all signatory countries to take full effect. President Lula also signed a decree establishing bilateral safeguard mechanisms to protect sensitive sectors, particularly agriculture, from potential adverse impacts of the agreement.

item.title: Trump ataca opositor e diz que pessoas com dificuldade de aprendizagem não deveriam ser presidente
item.description: Donald Trump criticized Gavin Newsom, the Governor of California and a potential presidential candidate, stating that people with learning difficulties should not be president, referencing Newsom's dyslexia. Trump called Newsom 'stupid' and mistakenly referred to him as 'president of the United States'. The National Center for Learning Disabilities in the U.S. condemned the remarks, stating that dyslexia does not impair intelligence, judgment, or leadership capacity.

item.title: Crusoé: PT antecipa narrativa que usará contra Flávio
item.description: The PT anticipated the narrative it will use against Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections, seeking to associate him with the continuation of his father Jair Bolsonaro’s political project. The information was disclosed in a resolution published on the party’s national website on Monday, April 16.

item.title: Motta diz que Câmara não fará discussão 'atropelada' sobre PEC da escala 6x1
item.description: Chamber President Hugo Motta stated that the debate on the PEC to reduce the workweek to four days will not be rushed, stressing the need to assess economic impacts and build consensus among workers, government, and the productive sector. He argued that processing the proposal as a constitutional amendment, rather than a regular bill, forces negotiation, and confirmed it will be a legislative priority in 2026 despite the election year.

item.title: Vídeo: Lançado Guia da Candidata, com informações políticas para mulheres
item.description: The Federal Senate launched the Candidacy Guide, a document produced by the Special Prosecutor’s Office for Women, containing information on election deadlines, party affiliation, women’s political rights, ineligibility, incompatibility, and political violence. The guide aims to increase women’s participation in positions of power.

item.title: Caso Ruy Ferraz: Tarcísio sanciona lei que amplia serviço de escolta a autoridades ameaçadas
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law expanding security and escort services for authorities, former officials, and their relatives threatened by organized crime, in response to the Ruy Ferraz case. The measure aims to ensure full protection for those facing threats due to their actions against organized crime.

item.title: Motta afirma que PEC garante melhor debate sobre fim da escala 6 X 1
item.description: House Speaker Hugo Motta argues that processing the workweek reduction via Constitutional Amendment (PEC) ensures a broader, more deliberate debate through multiple stages — CCJ, special committee, and plenary — rejecting the government’s push to fast-track via urgent bill. He emphasizes the measure’s significant economic impact requires societal consensus and warns against rushed approval in an election year, despite the Lula administration’s push for a first-semester vote to gain political advantage.

item.title: Por ordem de Lula, Planalto remarca assinatura de decretos do ECA Digital
item.description: The federal government rescheduled the signing of three decrees enabling the immediate implementation of the Digital ECA for 3 p.m. on Wednesday (March 18), after President Lula canceled the Tuesday morning ceremony to request detailed explanations of the regulation. The decrees establish the National Center for Child and Adolescent Protection under the Federal Police and restructure the ANPD as a regulatory agency overseeing the law’s application. The move is seen as a key achievement of Lula’s third term, despite the disruption caused by the last-minute cancellation.

item.title: Paulo Paim destaca importância de criar Frente Parlamentar pela Paz Mundial
item.description: Senator Paulo Paim (PT-RS) advocated for the approval of Resolution Project PRS 45/2025, which establishes the Parliamentary Front for World Peace, stressing the urgency due to over 100 global conflicts, including Ukraine, Iran, Israel, Gaza Strip, and African nations, and emphasizing that the most vulnerable — children, elderly, women — are always the primary victims. He also highlighted the importance of the Digital Statute for Children and Adolescents (Law No. 15.211), signed by Lula, as a protective — not censoring — measure to ensure safety in digital spaces for minors.

item.title: A nova vitória de Gilberto Kassab em São Paulo
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, succeeded in keeping Felício Ramuth as the vice-presidential candidate on Tarcísio de Freitas' re-election ticket, thwarting the PL's attempt to nominate André do Prado for the position. Kassab is expected to resign as São Paulo's government secretary by March 31 to focus on party preparations for the 2026 elections. The decision was based on the PSD's strong presence in the state legislature and its large number of affiliated mayors across São Paulo.

item.title: Presidente da CPMI do INSS quer prorrogação dos trabalhos e defende restrição de acesso aos dados
item.description: The president of the INSS CPMI, Senator Carlos Viana, advocates for a 60-day extension of the committee’s work to analyze relevant documents and information, having filed a writ of security with the STF to ensure continuity. He supports restricting access to seized confidential data, such as intimate videos of banker Daniel Vorcaro, to protect the investigation from leaks or errors that could invalidate evidence, and plans to invite representatives from banks like Crefisa, BMG, and C6 to collaborate in uncovering ties with the INSS and proposing legislative reforms against abusive fees.

item.title: Filipe Martins apresenta recurso à condenação no STF
item.description: The defense of Filipe Martins, former advisor to Jair Bolsonaro’s presidency, filed an appeal at the STF against his conviction for coup d’état, alleging false evidence from the Federal Police and omissions in the ruling. The appeal challenges the STF’s jurisdiction due to lack of privileged forum and highlights contradictions in geolocation data and witness testimony, including Brigadier Batista Junior’s denial of ever knowing Martins.

item.title: Quebra de sigilos esvazia suspeitas e Planalto vê Lulinha fora do escândalo do INSS
item.description: The lifting of secrecy ordered by the INSS CPI found no evidence implicating Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, leading the government to consider the case clarified. Ministers and aides state there is no direct or indirect link to the scheme of improper deductions in INSS payments, though they fear political exploitation by the opposition in an election year. The defense maintains that Lulinha’s 2024 trip to Portugal was related to his niece’s medicinal treatment and that no financial benefits were received from Antônio Carlos Camilo Antunes, the 'INSS bald man'.

item.title: 57% desaprovam o governo Lula no RS, diz pesquisa
item.description: A Real Time Big Data survey conducted from March 14 to 16, 2026 in Rio Grande do Sul shows that 57% of voters disapprove of President Lula's government, while 41% approve. Nearly half (46%) rated his administration as 'bad' or 'terrible', compared to only 30% who rated it 'good' or 'excellent'. The poll interviewed 1,500 people with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: Filho de Fux foi a camarote VIP de Vorcaro na Sapucaí
item.description: Rodrigo Fux, son of Supreme Court Justice Luiz Fux, was spotted in the VIP box of Daniel Vorcaro during the Marquês de Sapucaí parade in 2025. His presence drew attention due to his familial connection to a high-ranking judiciary figure and the context of ostentatious display during Carnival.

item.title: Pesquisa mostra empate entre 4 candidatos ao Senado no RS
item.description: The Real Time Big Data poll shows a technical tie among four Senate candidates in Rio Grande do Sul when Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) is included: he, Manuela D'Ávila (Psol), Marcel Van Hattem (Novo), and Sanderson (PL) each have 16%. Without Leite, Manuela and Van Hattem lead with 18% each, followed by Sanderson (17%) and Paulo Pimenta (PT) at 13%. The survey, conducted March 14–16, 2026, has a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: Lula cancela evento de lançamento do ECA Digital para revisar decreto
item.description: President Lula canceled the ECA Digital launch event to conduct a more detailed technical review of the decree, requesting an additional 24 hours despite the text being consensual among ministers. The ceremony has been rescheduled for Wednesday afternoon (18) at the Planalto Palace. The decree regulates digital system architecture to enhance minor safety, requiring big techs to block access to prohibited content and remove illegal material more swiftly, under ANPD oversight.

item.title: Democracia exige vigilância constante e Judiciário forte, diz Fachin
item.description: Supreme Court President Minister Edson Fachin stated that democracy is not a permanent gift and requires active and constant vigilance, along with a strong and independent Judiciary to uphold majority rule and fundamental rights. He emphasized institutional strength, separation of powers, and the need to reinforce multilateral mechanisms amid global crises. Fachin also advocated for judicial self-restraint, reform of judges’ remuneration, and the elimination of so-called 'penduricalhos'.

item.title: O aceno de Fux apesar de votar por manter Moro como réu no STF
item.description: Minister Luiz Fux voted to keep Senator Sergio Moro as a defendant in the STF for slander against Gilmar Mendes, but expressed support for deepening the investigation into the video's origin and Moro's prior knowledge. The First Chamber of the STF unanimously rejected Moro's appeal, upholding the indictment. Fux followed the rapporteur Minister Cármen Lúcia, emphasizing that the video dissemination issue should be subject to procedural instruction.

item.title: O Brasil tem que cuidar de sua democracia
item.description: Journalist and social scientist Maria Inês Nassif emphasizes the need for Brazil to safeguard its democracy, stressing the importance of defending institutions and the rule of law. She argues that the current context demands vigilance and civic participation to prevent democratic setbacks, particularly in the face of attempts to delegitimize the electoral process. Defending democracy, she says, must be a national priority, above partisan disputes.

item.title: Filipe Martins apresenta recurso contra condenação na ação do golpe de Estado
item.description: The defense of Filipe Martins, former advisor for International Affairs of the Presidency of the Republic, filed a motion for clarification (embargos de declaração) against his conviction in the criminal action investigating the 'núcleo 2' role in the alleged coup attempt in Brazil between 2022 and 2023. The appeal was submitted on Tuesday, the 17th, seeking to clarify aspects of the judicial decision. Martins was convicted by the First Section of the Superior Court of Justice for participation in anti-democratic acts.

item.title: PT diz que Flávio é 'marcado' por rachadinha e enriquecimento 'incompatível com vida pública'
item.description: The PT approved a political resolution accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) of being 'marked' by the 'rachadinha' scheme and illicit enrichment, stating such conduct is incompatible with public office. The document, endorsed by the party's leadership, aims to guide the PT's electoral strategy for 2026, positioning the senator as a central target of ethical and political criticism.

item.title: CPI do Crime vota convocações de Valdemar, ACM Neto e Martha Graeff
item.description: The Organized Crime CPI will vote on summonses for Valdemar da Costa Neto (PL), ACM Neto, and Martha Graeff, along with requests for bank secrecy breaks and testimonies from Paulo Sérgio Neves and Vladimir Timerman. The requests are tied to investigations into campaign donations, bank fraud in Operation Compliance Zero, and irregularities in consigned credit. Senator Humberto Costa (PT) is the main sponsor of the summonses against Valdemar and ACM Neto, citing links to Fabiano Zettel and Banco Master.

item.title: Senado lança 'Guia da Candidata' para orientar mulheres nas eleições
item.description: The Senate launched the 'Guia da Candidata,' a publication offering guidance to women on pre-candidacy, registration, financing, communication, and digital security to confront gender-based political violence and barriers in the 2026 elections. Led by Senator Augusta Brito (PT-CE), the initiative supports women candidates from diverse backgrounds — including Black, trans, Indigenous, rural, and peripheral communities — and their campaign teams, promoting autonomy and ethical participation. Senator Eliziane Gama (PSD-MA) highlighted the underreporting of violence against women and low awareness about the need to report such incidents.

item.title: Tarcísio, Tebet e Alckmin na disputa pelo espólio eleitoral do PSDB
item.description: Although still existing, the PSDB is politically in accelerated decline, with its electoral base in São Paulo estimated between 4% and 7% according to a 2026 Datafolha poll. This 'electoral inheritance' is being contested by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans), who seeks to absorb the party into his coalition to secure a first-round victory, and by the Lulista camp, which views the tucano legacy as a symbolic asset in the state. Tarcísio’s recent meeting with Aécio Neves and Paulo Serra, referencing Mário Covas, signals an attempt to co-opt the party’s structure to strengthen his candidacy.

item.title: Tarcisio sanciona lei que obriga execução do hino nacional nas escolas
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law making the vocal performance of the national anthem mandatory in all public and private elementary and high schools in São Paulo. The measure aims to strengthen national symbols and civic education among students.

item.title: 59% aprovam governo Eduardo Leite no RS, diz pesquisa
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows 59% of voters in Rio Grande do Sul approve of Governor Eduardo Leite’s (PSD) administration, while 36% disapprove. Health is the top priority for the next term (22%), followed by infrastructure (18%) and public security (16%). The survey of 1,500 respondents, conducted March 14–16, 2026, has a 95% confidence level and a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: PT diz que Flávio é ‘marcado’ por rachadinha e enriquecimento ‘incompatível com vida pública’
item.description: The PT’s national executive approved a resolution labeling Senator Flavio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) as 'marked' by allegations of 'rachadinha' and illicit enrichment incompatible with public office, linking him to the Banco Master scandal and the Bolsonaro authoritarian project. The move follows polls showing Flavio technically tied with Lula in second-round simulations, prompting the PT to launch early attacks ahead of the official campaign. The party claims Flavio has never introduced a meaningful project for national development.

item.title: Zucco lidera para o governo do RS, diz pesquisa
item.description: Luciano Zucco (PL) leads the race for Rio Grande do Sul governor in all first-round scenarios, with 31% to 36% voting intentions, followed by Juliana Brizola (PDT) and Edegar Pretto (PT). In second-round simulations, Zucco maintains an advantage over both, with 40% against Brizola and 43% against Pretto. The highest rejection rates are for Edegar Pretto (39%), followed by Brizola (38%) and Zucco (36%).

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei de combate à violência contra a mulher nas universidades de SP
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law requiring universities in São Paulo to adopt a protocol against violence toward women, covering prevention, victim support, and perpetrator accountability. The law takes effect in 90 days, with partial vetoes on measures such as perpetrator removal and victim leave excuses, citing concerns over university autonomy.

item.title: Federação do PT aciona TSE contra PL e irmãos Bolsonaro por vinculação com criminalidade
item.description: The Brazil of Hope federation (PT, PCdoB and PV) filed an injunction with the TSE against the PL and Bolsonaro brothers Flávio and Carlos for alleged premature electoral propaganda, claiming three Instagram videos spread lies and decontextualized information to falsely link Lula and the PT to criminal organizations. The videos accuse the government of protecting criminal factions, link the PT to the PCC, and attack Minister Gleisi Hoffmann, potentially using AI-generated synthetic images. The defendants have not responded to requests for comment.

item.title: Kassab aposta na rejeição e telhados de vidro de Lula e Flávio
item.description: Gilberto Kassab of the PSD states that the announcement of the candidate aimed at attracting the electorate divided between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro will occur by the end of the month, indicating the choice will fall on Ratinho Jr., whose name may undergo a rebranding. He keeps the name confidential but confirms the trend of supporting the Paranaense politician.

item.title: Quaest: Maioria já tem voto definido para eleição à Presidência
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll shows that 56% of Brazilian voters have already decided their presidential vote for October, considering it definitive. The most convinced voters support Lula (67%) and Flávio Bolsonaro (63%), while those most likely to change their minds back Aldo Rebelo (74%) and Romeu Zema (67%). Men are more certain (62%) than women (49%), and voters aged 16–34 are the most likely to change their vote (52%).

item.title: Fila do INSS diminui após 8 meses e fica abaixo de 3 milhões
item.description: The INSS backlog dropped below 3 million in March after eight consecutive months of increase, declining by 141,000 cases from February. INSS President Gilberto Waller Junior said the reduction occurred despite system instability and was driven by a record number of processed claims in a single month, with the goal of reducing the backlog to around 1.3 million by the end of 2026.

item.title: Diretriz de comunicação do PT incorpora caso Master como eixo de enfrentamento ao bolsonarismo
item.description: The PT approved a communication resolution for the 2026 pre-electoral campaign, establishing the comparison between Lula's and Bolsonaro's governments as the central axis, highlighting recent economic results, social policies, and petista administration actions. The party incorporates the Banco Master case as a symbol of the alleged articulation between financial interests and bolsonarism, questioning the inaction of the Central Bank under Roberto Campos Neto. It also directs criticism toward Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, defined as an 'expression of continuity' of the Bolsonarist project.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro faz novo pedido de prisão domiciliar humanitária ao STF
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro filed a new request with the Supreme Federal Court (STF) for him to serve house arrest on humanitarian grounds, citing a worsening medical condition. The request was submitted to Minister Alexandre de Moraes, the case's rapporteur. The move reflects ongoing efforts by Bolsonaro's legal team to alter his detention conditions.

item.title: Foragido do 8/1 usou fake news para obter refúgio político na Argentina
item.description: A Brazilian fugitive wanted for participating in the January 8, 2023 coup attempt used false claims about Lula’s alleged plans to legalize abortion and drugs to obtain political asylum in Argentina. Joel Borges Corrêa, a 47-year-old truck driver, told Argentina’s National Commission for Refugees (Conare) that the January 8 protests were not about contesting the election results, despite public evidence showing otherwise. The decision by Argentine authorities to grant him political asylum, reported by UOL in March 2026, follows earlier revelations of Bolsonarist fugitives seeking refuge in the country.

item.title: Federação do PT aciona TSE contra PL, Flávio e Carlos Bolsonaro por vinculação com criminalidade
item.description: The Brazil of Hope federation (PT, PCdoB, and PV) filed an injunction request with the TSE against the PL and brothers Flávio and Carlos Bolsonaro for alleged premature electoral propaganda, claiming three Instagram videos distort Brazil’s foreign policy and falsely link the Lula government and PT to organized crime, particularly the PCC. The videos, which use decontextualized audio, manipulated headlines, and allegedly AI-generated synthetic imagery, aim to create a false perception that the government protects criminal factions. The defendants have not responded to the allegations.
